# SRC013 - Bandit-Based Experimentation System for Online Advertising

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Measurement |
| Authors / source | Geng et al. |
| Year | 2021 |
| Source type | AAAI applied system paper |
| Quality | Medium-High |
| URL | [https://cdn.aaai.org/ojs/17775/17775-13-21269-1-2-20210518.pdf](https://cdn.aaai.org/ojs/17775/17775-13-21269-1-2-20210518.pdf) |

## Bias / limitation

Applied platform context; useful for architecture more than universal law.

## Core takeaway

Bandits can allocate creative traffic dynamically but need clear reward and system design.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use for creative allocation loops after basic tracking is stable.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC013
- What it changes about our strategy:
- What it proves:
- What it does not prove:
- Ad tactic suggested:
- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
